sessions.transcript: User: Keep SQLite for local development for now. Agent: Noted, SQLite is only used for local development.
User: Production is moving to PostgreSQL for parity and safer migrations. Agent: Recording PostgreSQL as the production database choice.

facts.label: SQLite local only
facts.content: SQLite is only used for local development.

facts.label: PostgreSQL production
facts.content: PostgreSQL is the production database for parity and safer migrations.

sessions.transcript: User: JWT tokens expire after 15 minutes today. Agent: Captured, JWT tokens expire after 15 minutes.
User: Extend auth expiry to 1 hour now that refresh rotation is stable. Agent: Updated, JWT tokens now expire after 1 hour.

facts.label: JWT expiry 15 minutes
facts.content: JWT tokens expire after 15 minutes.

facts.label: JWT expiry 1 hour
facts.content: JWT tokens now expire after 1 hour.

sessions.transcript: User: Redis handles session caching because TTL support is simple. Agent: Saving Redis as the session cache backend.
User: Move session caching to KeyDB for active-active failover. Agent: Recording KeyDB as the new session cache backend.

facts.label: Redis session cache
facts.content: Redis handles session caching because TTL support is simple.

facts.label: KeyDB session cache
facts.content: KeyDB now handles session caching for active-active failover.

sessions.transcript: User: Start with PostgreSQL trigram search while traffic is low. Agent: Logged, PostgreSQL trigram search is the initial search engine.
User: Switch product search to OpenSearch for better ranking controls. Agent: Recording OpenSearch as the new product search engine.

facts.label: Trigram search
facts.content: PostgreSQL trigram search was the initial search engine.

facts.label: OpenSearch search
facts.content: OpenSearch is the current product search engine for better ranking controls.

sessions.transcript: User: Keep the frontend as a React SPA initially. Agent: Recorded, the frontend starts as a React SPA.
User: Move the frontend to Next.js for server rendering and routing. Agent: Recording Next.js as the current frontend framework.

facts.label: React SPA
facts.content: The frontend started as a React SPA.

facts.label: Next.js frontend
facts.content: The current frontend framework is Next.js for server rendering and routing.

sessions.transcript: User: Run analytics as a nightly batch while volumes stay low. Agent: Saved, analytics run as a nightly batch.
User: Move analytics to streaming ingestion for near-real-time dashboards. Agent: Recording streaming ingestion as the current analytics pipeline.

facts.label: Nightly analytics batch
facts.content: Analytics initially ran as a nightly batch.

facts.label: Streaming analytics
facts.content: Analytics now use streaming ingestion for near-real-time dashboards.

sessions.transcript: User: Start notifications with email only to keep the first release simple. Agent: Noted, email is the only notification channel in v1.
User: Add Slack notifications for ops alerts alongside email. Agent: Recording email plus Slack as the current notification setup.

facts.label: Email only notifications
facts.content: Email was the only notification channel in v1.

facts.label: Email and Slack notifications
facts.content: Notifications now use both email and Slack for ops alerts.

sessions.transcript: User: Stripe is the billing provider for launch. Agent: Logged, Stripe handles billing at launch.
User: Migrate billing to Paddle for global tax handling. Agent: Recording Paddle as the current billing provider.

facts.label: Stripe billing
facts.content: Stripe handled billing at launch.

facts.label: Paddle billing
facts.content: Paddle is the current billing provider for global tax handling.

sessions.transcript: User: Deploy the API to ECS first because the team already knows it. Agent: Saving ECS as the original API deployment target.
User: Move the API to Cloud Run for simpler autoscaling. Agent: Recording Cloud Run as the current API deployment target.

facts.label: ECS deployment
facts.content: The API originally deployed to ECS because the team already knew it.

facts.label: Cloud Run deployment
facts.content: The API now deploys to Cloud Run for simpler autoscaling.

sessions.transcript: User: Use Algolia for docs search in the first release. Agent: Recorded, Algolia powers docs search initially.
User: Replace Algolia with local BM25 search to cut recurring cost. Agent: Recording local BM25 as the current docs search engine.

facts.label: Algolia docs search
facts.content: Algolia powered docs search initially.

facts.label: BM25 docs search
facts.content: Local BM25 now powers docs search to cut recurring cost.

sessions.transcript: User: Start with role-based access only. Agent: Logged, access control is role-based only in the initial version.
User: Add attribute-based rules for region and customer tier. Agent: Recording role plus attribute-based rules as the current access model.

facts.label: RBAC only
facts.content: Access control started as role-based access only.

facts.label: RBAC and ABAC
facts.content: Access control now uses role-based and attribute-based rules for region and customer tier.

sessions.transcript: User: Skip offline mode for the first mobile release. Agent: Noted, the first mobile release has no offline support.
User: Queue edits offline and sync them later. Agent: Recording offline edit queues as the current mobile behavior.

facts.label: No offline support
facts.content: The first mobile release had no offline support.

facts.label: Offline edit queue
facts.content: Mobile now queues edits offline and syncs them later.

sessions.transcript: User: Offer CSV export only for the first release. Agent: Logged, CSV is the only export format initially.
User: Add Parquet export for data warehouse sync. Agent: Recording CSV and Parquet as the current export formats.

facts.label: CSV only export
facts.content: CSV was the only export format initially.

facts.label: CSV and Parquet export
facts.content: Exports now support both CSV and Parquet for data warehouse sync.

sessions.transcript: User: Do not auto-rollback on deploy failures yet. Agent: Noted, deployments do not auto-rollback initially.
User: Auto-rollback when 5xx error rate spikes after deploy. Agent: Recording auto-rollback on 5xx spikes as the current policy.

facts.label: No auto rollback
facts.content: Deployments did not auto-rollback initially.

facts.label: Auto rollback on 5xx
facts.content: Deployments now auto-rollback when 5xx error rate spikes after deploy.

sessions.transcript: User: Keep logs for 30 days at first. Agent: Saved, log retention is 30 days initially.
User: Keep audit logs for 90 days for compliance. Agent: Recording 90 day audit log retention as the current policy.

facts.label: 30 day log retention
facts.content: Log retention was 30 days initially.

facts.label: 90 day audit retention
facts.content: Audit logs now retain for 90 days for compliance.

sessions.transcript: User: Use Celery with Redis as the background job system. Agent: Logged, Celery with Redis is the original queue backend.
User: Move workflows to Temporal for retries and visibility. Agent: Recording Temporal as the current workflow backend.

facts.label: Celery and Redis
facts.content: Celery with Redis was the original background job system.

facts.label: Temporal workflows
facts.content: Temporal is the current workflow backend for retries and visibility.

sessions.transcript: User: Write migrations manually at first. Agent: Saved, schema migrations are manual initially.
User: Switch to Alembic autogenerate with manual review. Agent: Recording Alembic autogenerate plus manual review as the current migration flow.

facts.label: Manual migrations
facts.content: Schema migrations were manual initially.

facts.label: Alembic autogenerate
facts.content: Schema migrations now use Alembic autogenerate with manual review.

sessions.transcript: User: Keep onboarding as a long single form first. Agent: Logged, onboarding starts as a long single form.
User: Redesign onboarding as a step-by-step wizard. Agent: Recording a step-by-step onboarding wizard as the current flow.

facts.label: Long-form onboarding
facts.content: Onboarding started as a long single form.

facts.label: Wizard onboarding
facts.content: Onboarding is now a step-by-step wizard.

sessions.transcript: User: Feature flags are environment variables for now. Agent: Saved, feature flags are environment variables initially.
User: Move feature flags into an internal control plane service. Agent: Recording the internal flag service as the current feature flag system.

facts.label: Env-var flags
facts.content: Feature flags were environment variables initially.

facts.label: Internal flag service
facts.content: Feature flags now live in an internal control plane service.

sessions.transcript: User: Poll upstream systems every 15 minutes first. Agent: Logged, sync uses 15 minute polling initially.
User: Prefer webhooks with polling fallback for missed events. Agent: Recording webhooks plus polling fallback as the current sync strategy.

facts.label: Polling sync
facts.content: Sync initially used 15 minute polling.

facts.label: Webhook sync
facts.content: Sync now prefers webhooks with polling fallback for missed events.

sessions.transcript: User: Start with logs only while traffic is low. Agent: Saved, observability starts with logs only.
User: Add traces and service-level objectives. Agent: Recording traces and SLOs as the current observability stack.

facts.label: Logs only
facts.content: Observability started with logs only.

facts.label: Traces and SLOs
facts.content: Observability now includes traces and service-level objectives.

sessions.transcript: User: Store uploads in S3 Standard first. Agent: Logged, uploads go to S3 Standard initially.
User: Move cold uploads to S3 Intelligent-Tiering. Agent: Recording S3 Intelligent-Tiering for cold uploads as the current storage policy.

facts.label: S3 Standard uploads
facts.content: Uploads went to S3 Standard initially.

facts.label: S3 Intelligent-Tiering uploads
facts.content: Cold uploads now use S3 Intelligent-Tiering.

sessions.transcript: User: Escalate support tickets manually during business hours only. Agent: Saved, support escalations are manual and business-hours only.
User: Auto-page the on-call engineer for Sev1 incidents. Agent: Recording automatic on-call paging for Sev1 incidents as the current policy.

facts.label: Manual business-hour escalation
facts.content: Support escalations were manual and business-hours only.

facts.label: Auto-page Sev1
facts.content: Sev1 incidents now automatically page the on-call engineer.

sessions.transcript: User: Accept CSV uploads first for ingestion. Agent: Logged, ingestion accepts CSV uploads initially.
User: Add NDJSON ingestion for streaming imports. Agent: Recording NDJSON as the current additional ingestion format.

facts.label: CSV ingestion
facts.content: Ingestion accepted CSV uploads initially.

facts.label: NDJSON ingestion
facts.content: Ingestion now also accepts NDJSON for streaming imports.

scenarios.title: Context Assembly: Decision + Reason Co-appearance
scenarios.description: Verify that when querying a decision, supporting reasons are also returned (support coverage)

sessions.transcript: User: We need PostgreSQL because ACID compliance is critical. Agent: Recorded decision to use PostgreSQL with ACID requirements.

facts.label: PostgreSQL chosen
facts.content: Chose PostgreSQL for ACID compliance and transactional guarantees

facts.label: ACID compliance required
facts.content: ACID compliance is critical for maintaining data integrity in financial transactions

scenarios.title: Context Assembly: Contradiction Both-Sides Coverage
scenarios.description: Verify that contradictions surface both the old and new decision with the edge between them

sessions.transcript: User: We're using SQLite for now. Agent: Noted SQLite as current database.
User: We've switched to PostgreSQL because SQLite has concurrency issues. Agent: Recorded update to PostgreSQL due to scalability needs.

facts.label: SQLite original choice
facts.content: Originally chose SQLite for simplicity in local development

facts.label: PostgreSQL updated choice
facts.content: Updated to PostgreSQL for production deployment and concurrent write support

facts.label: Concurrency limitation found
facts.content: SQLite cannot handle concurrent writes, which became a blocker for scaling

scenarios.title: Context Assembly: Multi-Hop Dependency Chain
scenarios.description: Verify that complex dependency chains preserve full reasoning context (decision → requirement → underlying fact)

sessions.transcript: User: We need FastAPI because we require real-time WebSocket support for concurrent connections. Agent: Captured decision and reasoning chain.

facts.label: FastAPI backend selected
facts.content: Chose FastAPI as the web framework for async-first architecture

facts.label: Real-time communication requirement
facts.content: Application requires real-time WebSocket connections for live data updates

facts.label: Concurrent connection handling
facts.content: System must support thousands of concurrent WebSocket connections with efficient async I/O

sessions.transcript: User: During the security review, we decided not to store raw API keys in application logs. Agent: Recorded, raw API keys must not be logged.
User: The review also rejected shared admin accounts; every privileged action needs named ownership. Agent: Noted, shared admin accounts are rejected.
User: For that security review item, we landed per-user break-glass accounts with audit trails. Agent: Updated, break-glass access is per-user and audited.

facts.label: No raw API key logs
facts.content: Raw API keys must not be stored in application logs.

facts.label: Shared admins rejected
facts.content: Shared admin accounts were rejected because privileged actions need named ownership.

facts.label: Audited break-glass access
facts.content: Break-glass access now uses per-user accounts with audit trails.

sessions.transcript: User: Do not enable one-click refunds until abuse monitoring is in place. Agent: Captured, one-click refunds are blocked until abuse monitoring exists.
User: For risk checks, start with manual review on high-value refunds. Agent: Noted, high-value refunds require manual review.
User: Now route high-risk refunds through the rules engine and keep manual review as fallback. Agent: Updated, high-risk refunds use the rules engine with manual fallback.

facts.label: One-click refunds blocked
facts.content: One-click refunds must not be enabled until abuse monitoring is in place.

facts.label: Manual refund review
facts.content: High-value refunds initially required manual review.

facts.label: Rules engine refund checks
facts.content: High-risk refunds now go through the rules engine with manual review as fallback.

sessions.transcript: User: We cannot email full data exports directly to users. Agent: Recorded, full data exports must not be sent over email.
User: Data exports should be delivered through signed download links that expire after 24 hours. Agent: Saved, exports use 24-hour signed links.
User: The latest privacy-export stance is signed links plus admin approval for enterprise tenants. Agent: Updated, enterprise exports require admin approval and signed links.

facts.label: No emailed full exports
facts.content: Full data exports must not be emailed directly to users.

facts.label: Signed export links
facts.content: Data exports are delivered through signed download links that expire after 24 hours.

facts.label: Enterprise export approval
facts.content: Enterprise data exports now require admin approval and signed download links.

sessions.transcript: User: Do not auto-promote evaluation winners to production. Agent: Noted, evaluation winners are not auto-promoted.
User: Model releases require offline eval pass plus a canary window. Agent: Saved, model releases need offline eval and canary validation.
User: Latest model rollout gate adds product-manager approval after the canary. Agent: Updated, PM approval follows canary before full model rollout.

facts.label: No model auto-promotion
facts.content: Evaluation winners must not be auto-promoted to production.

facts.label: Offline eval and canary
facts.content: Model releases require offline evaluation plus a canary window.

facts.label: PM approval rollout gate
facts.content: Full model rollout now requires product-manager approval after the canary.

sessions.transcript: User: We are not launching payments in Brazil until tax invoicing is ready. Agent: Recorded, Brazil payments launch is blocked by tax invoicing.
User: Start regional rollout with Canada because compliance is already reviewed. Agent: Noted, Canada is first for regional rollout.
User: The latest regional plan keeps Canada first and adds Brazil only after invoicing certification. Agent: Updated regional launch plan.

facts.label: Brazil payments blocked
facts.content: Payments must not launch in Brazil until tax invoicing is ready.

facts.label: Canada first launch
facts.content: Canada is the first regional rollout because compliance has been reviewed.

facts.label: Brazil after certification
facts.content: Brazil payments launch only after invoicing certification while Canada remains first.

failure_protocol: If Waggle token reduction is under 15 percent, inspect whether graph serialization or context assembly is offsetting compression gains.
If the tuned baseline matches Waggle on retrieval quality, frame the result as efficiency and structure first rather than retrieval superiority.
If temporal queries do not separate systems, audit whether the corpus actually requires temporal reasoning before expanding claims.
If multi-session change queries are inconclusive, expand that slice before broadening the whole pilot corpus.
